expectations.text: container-changelog.md exists and starts with '## X.Y.Z' where X.Y.Z is a valid semver version
expectations.evidence: File exists, line 1 is '## 0.21.0' which is valid semver.

expectations.text: container-changelog.md contains a '**Full Changelog**' link using the ngrok-operator- tag prefix
expectations.evidence: Line 2: '**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/ngrok/ngrok-operator/compare/ngrok-operator-0.20.3...ngrok-operator-0.21.0' uses the ngrok-operator- tag prefix for both from and to tags.

expectations.text: container-changelog.md uses Keep-a-Changelog sections (### Added, ### Changed, ### Fixed, etc.) with no empty sections
expectations.evidence: File contains ### Breaking Changes (1 entry), ### Added (2 entries), ### Changed (7 entries), ### Fixed (7 entries). All sections have content; no empty sections.

expectations.text: container-changelog.md entries have correct attribution format: 'by @author in [#NNN](https://github.com/ngrok/ngrok-operator/pull/NNN)'
expectations.evidence: All 18 entries follow the pattern. Example: 'by @sabrina-ngrok in [#779](https://github.com/ngrok/ngrok-operator/pull/779)'. Verified all PR numbers match their URLs. Authors match ground truth data for all 18 PRs.

expectations.text: container-changelog.md includes PR #799 (CloudEndpoint IsNotFound handling) and PR #803 (misc quick wins) since these are new for container
expectations.evidence: Line 24: 'Fixed CloudEndpoint controller to use ReconcileStatus for IsNotFound handling...in [#799]'. Line 25: 'Fixed data race in ChannelHealthChecker.Alive()...in [#803]'. Both present with accurate descriptions.

expectations.text: container-changelog.md does NOT include PR #781 or PR #777 since these have new_for:[] (already released)
expectations.evidence: Grep for '#781' and '#777' in container-changelog.md returned no matches. The summary.md correctly lists both as skipped: 'PR #777 (SECURITY.md) - docs only, not new for any component' and 'PR #781 (gateway/ingress status fixes) - already released in 0.20.3'.

expectations.text: helm-changelog.md exists and starts with version metadata lines ('- Update ngrok-operator image version to')
expectations.evidence: File exists but starts with '## 0.23.0' and '**Full Changelog**: ...' before the metadata lines. The '- Update ngrok-operator image version to `0.21.0`' line appears on line 5, not at the start. The assertion requires the file to start with those metadata lines.

expectations.text: crds-changelog.md exists since there are 4 PRs with new_for containing helm_crds
expectations.evidence: File exists and contains entries for all 4 helm_crds PRs: #779 (Breaking Changes - endpointURI rename), #772 (Added - wide output columns), #792 (Fixed - ProxyProtocolVersion enum), #803 (Fixed - scaffolding comments). Matches ground truth exactly.

expectations.text: versions.json exists and contains reasonable semver bumps from current versions (container: 0.20.3, helm: 0.22.2, crds: 0.2.1)
expectations.evidence: versions.json contains: container 0.21.0 (minor bump from 0.20.3, justified by breaking change #779 and new features), helm_operator 0.23.0 (minor bump from 0.22.2), helm_crds 0.3.0 (minor bump from 0.2.1, justified by breaking change #779). All are forward bumps with correct semver semantics.

expectations.text: Authors are GitHub usernames (e.g., @alex-bezek, @jonstacks, @sabrina-ngrok) not email addresses or full names
expectations.evidence: All entries across all changelogs use @-prefixed GitHub usernames: @alex-bezek, @jonstacks, @sabrina-ngrok, @app/copilot-swe-agent. No email addresses or full names found. All usernames verified against ground truth data.

claims.claim: 22 PRs merged since the last release tags
claims.evidence: Ground truth data contains exactly 22 PRs total.

Container changelog has exactly 18 entries. All 18 PR numbers match the ground truth new_for:container list: #768, #772, #773, #779, #782, #784, #785, #786, #787, #788, #789, #791, #792, #793, #794, #795, #799, #803.
CRDs changelog contains entries for #772, #779, #792, #803 -- matching ground truth exactly.
Helm changelog contains only #773 -- matching ground truth (the only PR with new_for containing helm_operator).
Ground truth shows #779 has_breaking_changes: true. The changelog correctly categorizes it under ### Breaking Changes.
Ground truth shows all four have new_for: []. Summary correctly lists them as skipped with accurate reasons.

Cross-referenced all 18 container changelog entries against ground truth author fields. Every attribution matches: alex-bezek (7 PRs), jonstacks (6 PRs), sabrina-ngrok (3 PRs), app/copilot-swe-agent (3 PRs). Note: andrew-harris-at-ngrok was listed as a real author in the grading context but has no PRs in this release.
PR #785 body in ground truth confirms: 'Updates from go 1.25.7 to 1.26.1'.

suggestions.assertion: helm-changelog.md exists and starts with version metadata lines ('- Update ngrok-operator image version to')
suggestions.reason: This assertion penalizes the output for including a version header (## 0.23.0) and Full Changelog link before the metadata lines. However, this is consistent with the container-changelog.md format and arguably better. Consider whether the assertion should require the metadata lines to be present (anywhere) rather than requiring them at the very start.
No assertion checks whether the crds-changelog.md has the correct Full Changelog link format (using helm-chart-ngrok-crds- tag prefix). The output does include this correctly, but it goes unverified.

No assertion checks that the helm-changelog.md references the correct container image version (0.21.0) and CRDs dependency version (0.3.0). These cross-references are important for release correctness and the output handles them well.
No assertion verifies that PR descriptions are accurate to the source data. The output has high-quality summaries, but a hallucinated description would pass all current assertions.

eval_feedback.overall: Strong eval coverage on the container changelog. The helm-changelog assertion is arguably too prescriptive about format rather than content. Missing assertions around cross-component version consistency and description accuracy.
